reflection:
you survived the move. london to berlin yesterday with a body already cooked from monday's training, and you still made it onto the plane, did the stool samples, and ended the night journaling on a terrace where you could see the stars. that counts for more than the score sheet shows.

the pain creep is real though. glute, pelvic floor, coccyx all sitting at 4. evening pain pushed to 5. brain fog jumped to 5 yesterday when it had been 1 to 3 all week. five awakenings, only 3 out of 5 refreshed. you are walking into today already tired, and today is the IMD Berlin draw. that's the big one.

the line in your morning journal about Ivan, that he is always so fit and you have to show your weak side, that one is heavy. it's the old story showing up uninvited on a hard week. same gear as the Turkey worry in your evening dump. neither of them belongs in today's drive to IMD. park them.

one thing for today: pace IMD like you paced the move. urine sample, blood draw, hotel, lie down. do not stack errands on the back of it. your body has enough to do.

and eat earlier tonight if you can. 20:30 is late for you.
opener: you survived london to berlin yesterday. brain fog and pain trending up.
focus_for_today: pace IMD Berlin like you paced the move. one thing at a time, then lie down.
